_doc: rs2853669 (TERT promoter) multi-oracle panel config. Variant chr5:1,295,234 A→G in hg38 (TERT-strand: T→C). Cell type: K562 (TERT-positive, ENCODE-rich). Predictions verified on chorus v0.5.6 (commit f7c1c32). Ground truth from Helbig et al. 2017 (Oncotarget, PMC5620167). Note: Helbig used Bre80 / MCF-7 breast cells, not K562; cell-type transfer caveat.
prompt: Investigate the possible effects of rs2853669 on chromatin accessibility, promoter activity, TF binding, and histone modifications using appropriate models. Aggregate the predictions and provide me with a list of possible consequences.

alphagenome.track:
CAGE @ enhancer/variant site
strongest single AG signal

CAGE @ TERT TSS −0.04 / −0.16
RNA-seq @ TERT exons −0.026 to −0.029
(4/4 RNA-seq tracks ↓)

predictions: Minor G allele slightly closes chromatin per AG DNase (−0.07); ChromBPNet flat.
Predicted GABP binding change is tiny (~5%); AG sees DOWN, BPNet flat.
eRNA at the SNP site DOWN (AG CAGE −0.16, the strongest single signal).
TERT TSS CAGE and all 4 RNA-seq tracks directionally DOWN (−0.03 to −0.16).
Single-fragment MPRA at SNP site flat (LegNet K562 +0.003).

ground_truth.title: Ground truth (Helbig 2017)
bullets: Risk-associated minor C (= +strand G) allele preferentially binds GABPA by allele-specific ChIP-qPCR.
Risk haplotype reduces TERT promoter activity in luciferase assays in Bre80 (normal breast) and MCF-7 cells.
GABPA binding validated by ChIP in Bre80 cells (sc-22810-X antibody).
Functional dissection across 6 breast-cancer-risk TERT-promoter variants identified rs2853669 as functional.
Direction (↓) matches AG K562; magnitude (no fold-change reported, P<0.05) is consistent with the small effects predicted here.
Caveat: Helbig used breast cells; K562 chorus tracks are an indirect cell-type proxy.

igv_meta: TERT ±150 kb (canonical IGV from rs12740374 example; rs2853669-specific IGV TBD)
